title: Sour Ethiopian Brie Stew
description: A tangy, cheese-pull stew that marries creamy Brie with dashi, berbere, and fresh acidity for a lazy Sunday Ethiopian dinner.

ingredients.name: Brie cheese
ingredients.notes: cut into cubes, soft and meltable

instructions: Preheat a bamboo tray over medium heat and drizzle with olive oil.
Add onions and sauté until translucent.
Stir in garlic, ginger, and berbere spice until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in tomato sauce and vegetable broth, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add dashi extract and lemon juice, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes to develop acidity.
Gently fold in Brie cubes, allowing them to melt into the stew while keeping some chunks for pull.
Transfer the warm stew onto the bamboo tray, cover loosely with foil, and let rest for 5 minutes for the cheese to set.
Serve hot with a drizzle of extra olive oil, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
